key_insights.insight: Self-responsibility is key: believing anything is within reach if you choose it to be is a radical part of life.
key_insights.quote: I truly believe that everything I want, who I am, anything is within my reach if I choose it to be.

key_insights.insight: Focus is like a false multiplier in work, enabling you to double down on what's important.
key_insights.quote: Focus is like a false multiplier in work, right? When you have a plan and you have a focus and you can kind of go into what it is that you find important and double down on a very finite number of things, that's what propels you forward.

key_insights.insight: Drowning out noise and disregarding what people think requires single-minded focus.
key_insights.quote: There's just so much noise and you have to have like this single-minded focus on what you're doing.

key_insights.insight: Everything your customer says is true because it's true to them, therefore customer feedback should be ingested differently than decision-making feedback.
key_insights.quote: Everything your customer says is true because it's true to them.

key_insights.insight: Having personal board of directors, comprised of 5-10 people allows decision-making to be easier.
key_insights.quote: Personal board of directors... these five or seven people that you typically call, maybe it's 10.

key_insights.insight: One should make whoever is around them a mentor by asking questions.
key_insights.quote: You have to walk around asking questions. Because anyone who's going to be a good mentor probably doesn't have time to mentor you, first.

key_insights.insight: Grit, the characteristic of being clear about what you want and will find a way to get it, even with zero qualifications, is what makes a person truly successful.
key_insights.quote: I'm very clear about what I want and what I need, and I will find a way, whatever it is.I have zero qualifications to do any of that stuff. I will just make it happen.

key_insights.insight: When assessing a potential employee, look for a winning mentality and the ability to figure things out, prioritizing attitude over experience.
key_insights.quote: I have higher, much more for attitude over experience. I really want the people that come in with like a winning mentality, a figure it out mentality.

key_insights.insight: Be excellent at your current role before seeking opportunities elsewhere.
key_insights.quote: I'm looking at the 120% people, the people that are smashing it in the role that they're at now before they're going to go anywhere else.

key_insights.insight: Work-life balance is a personal responsibility, not an employer's, with flexibility being more important now.
key_insights.quote: Work-life balance is your problem. That's yours to figure out.

actionable_advice.advice: Focus on a finite number of things and double down.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify the most important tasks or goals, then dedicate your time and energy to those areas, minimizing distractions.

actionable_advice.advice: Develop the ability to separate a gut feeling from excitement.
actionable_advice.how_to: Pause, reflect, and ask yourself if the feeling is coming from a place in your heart or just an emotional, exciting reaction.

actionable_advice.advice: Cultivate a willingness to take risks.
actionable_advice.how_to: Start small, gradually increasing the size and scope of the risks you take. Analyze the potential downsides and develop contingency plans.
As the business grows, re-evaluate team members' roles and responsibilities, and if needed, make the difficult decision to move people out of the business for the long-term success of the business.
Figure out the problem, think of a unique solution, create a value proposition, and get it to others in a unique way
Reach people that want to learn, take what you've learned and those around you, and have an impactful conversation

memorable_stories: Emma's visualization of her dream home as a child and then realizing it as an adult.
Her early experiences raising her younger sisters while her mom worked.
Walking up to the founder in the gym who ripped off her designs.
Experiencing loss during her fertility journey.
contrarian_claims: Work-life balance is your problem, not your employer's.
You can't be a leader and a people-pleaser at the same time.
Hiring friends is a terrible bloody idea.
It's not just about skills, people and experiences, it's what the environment they are in.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - SKIMS Co-Founder (Emma Grede):  They're Lying About Work Life Balance! I Built The Kardashian Empire!  I Built SKIMS Without Fashion Knowledge!
